title: eriophyllum lanatum var. croceum
intro: Eriophyllum lanatum var. croceum, also known as Orange Woodsage or Western Yarrow, is a perennial herb native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps. The medicinal properties of Eriophyllum lanatum var. croceum are attributed to its high concentration of terpenes and flavonoids, which have been shown to promote digestive health, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions and respiratory infections.
